sections.paragraph:
προβιβάζω (Aristoph., Soph., Pla. et al.; PBad 39 II, 4 [IV a.d.]; Just., D. 90, 1) fut. 2 sg. προβιβάσεις Dt 6:7; 1 aor. προεβίβασα; pass. ptc. προβιβασθείς cause to come forward (Soph., Oed. Col. 180; Kaibel 947, 1) ἐκ τοῦ ὄχλου προεβίβασαν Ἀλέξανδρον they made Alexander come out from the crowd Ac 19:33 v.l. ἡ δὲ προβιβασθεῖσα ὑπὸ τῆς μητρός but she, pushed forward by her mother Mt 14:8 (here many prefer the mng. ‘incite, urge on [beforehand], prompt’. But προβιβ. εἴς τι in places like X., Mem. 1, 5, 1 εἰς ἐγκράτειαν, Pla., Prot. 328b εἰς ἀρετήν, Musonius p. 60, 5 H. νέους εἰς φιλοσοφίαν means ‘lead on to’, ‘train in’. Even more compelling is usage in the LXX, where the word=‘instruct, teach, inculcate’: Ex 35:34; Dt 6:7; Just., D. 90, 1 ἡμᾶς … προβίβασον ἐκ τῶν γραφῶν). ‘Coached’ may best express the mng.—DELG s.v. βαίνω. M-M. Spicq.

Aristoph Aristoph , V–IV b.c.—List 5

Soph Soph , V b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

PBad PBad = Veröffentlichungen aus der badischen Papyrussammlungen—List 4

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

fut. fut. = fut.

  1. sg. = singular

aor. aor. = aorist

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

ptc. ptc. = participle

Kaibel Kaibel = Epigrammata Graeca ex Lapidibus Conlecta, ed. GK.—List 3

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

mng. mng. = meaning(s)

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Musonius Musonius = Gaius Musonius Rufus, I a.d.—List 5

  1. H. = FHort, s. W-H.—List 1, beg.

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Spicq Spicq = CS., Lexique théologique du Nouveau Testament—Lists 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 866
